summary: Reviewed the bilingual PT/EN i18n feature (uncommitted diff on feat/mateushr-portfolio-redesign-space). 18 changed files + 6 new files scoped to: src/i18n/**, src/app/en/page.tsx, src/components/space/LocaleToggle.tsx, proxy.ts x-pathname forwarding, layout.tsx headers()/html lang flip, ProjectsCarousel selectDescription locale fallback, readme.ts bilingual parser, sync.ts description_pt/description_en plumbing, types/database.ts regenerated, and the 20260417200000_bilingual_descriptions SQL migration. No CRITICAL findings: no security holes in the x-pathname forwarding (forwardedHeaders.set() overwrites any request-supplied value; cookie-refresh rebuild reuses the same forwardedHeaders instance); sitemap.xml and robots.txt remain Static in the build output because metadata routes don't inherit root layout's headers() call; dict fallback in getDictionary is null-safe for untrusted locale input; i18n dictionaries are structurally equal; proxy matcher unchanged. WARNING / SUGGESTION level findings are catalogued below. ready_for_git_ops=true.

findings.description: aria-pressed is defined by WAI-ARIA for role=button, not role=link. Using it on a <Link> (rendered as <a>) is an ARIA contract mismatch — axe with strict rules (wcag2a aria-allowed-attr) can flag it. Functionally screen readers still announce state, but it's semantically noisy. This matches QA's P2 finding #1.
findings.fix: Replace aria-pressed with aria-current="page" on the currently-active side (drop aria-pressed on the inactive one). aria-current is the canonical pattern for same-site locale toggles rendered as links, and preserves middle-click / open-in-new-tab. Alternatively convert both to <button type="button"> that router.push — but aria-current is cheaper and keeps navigation semantics.

findings.description: pt.hero.messageMarker = "Uma mensagem da Terra" contradicts the documented design intent in src/i18n/types.ts:27 ("Kept in English on both sides — rendered as a display wordmark") and in src/components/space/GalaxyHeadline.tsx:11 ("The marker text itself stays in English on both sides (it's the wordmark motif of the design)"). en.ts keeps it as the English wordmark "A MESSAGE FROM EARTH". Either the copy is wrong or the docs are wrong. Matches QA P2 #2.
findings.fix: Decide intent with the user. Likely fix: set pt.hero.messageMarker = "A MESSAGE FROM EARTH" to align with en.ts and the documented wordmark rule (also matches the visual consistency that hreflang alternates imply). If the PT translation is deliberate, update the comments in types.ts and GalaxyHeadline.tsx to reflect that the marker translates.

findings.description: CAROUSEL_REPO_ORDER and fetchCarouselRepos() are duplicated verbatim across the PT and EN page files. Drift risk — if the order or select columns change, the two pages can silently diverge. Both are already locale-agnostic (ProjectsCarousel picks description_pt vs description_en downstream). Matches QA P2 #3.
findings.fix: Extract into src/lib/home/fetch-carousel.ts (or src/lib/repos/carousel.ts). Export CAROUSEL_REPO_ORDER and fetchCarouselRepos; call from both page files. Keeps the narrative order authoritative in one place.

findings.description: Confirmed dead code via grep: ProjectIndex imports IndexRow but is itself never imported; IndexRow is only referenced from ProjectIndex; SiteHeader and SiteFooter are unused by any active route (the space design replaced them). IndexRow still contains a hardcoded PT-BR string "Projeto em TypeScript — descrição em breve." (line 11) that would render in PT on /en if the component were ever re-adopted. SiteHeader contains hardcoded "projetos", "sobre", "contato" nav copy. Matches QA P2 #4.
findings.fix: Delete IndexRow.tsx, ProjectIndex.tsx, SiteHeader.tsx, SiteFooter.tsx (and their associated CSS if any) in a follow-up cleanup commit, or refactor them to accept a dict slice before re-introducing them to any route. Not blocking — they don't leak into the EN render path today because no page imports them.

findings.description: Both accept the full dict.contact slice (8 fields: corner, heading, emailLabel, subjectLabel, contentLabel, submit, back) but BackLink uses only dict.back and ContactLink uses only dict.corner. Not a bug — dict is a stable module-level object reference so it won't trigger re-renders — just an over-broad prop slice that makes the intent fuzzy.
findings.fix: Either (a) narrow the props to the single string each component needs (prop-drill only the label, e.g. label={dict.contact.back}) or (b) keep as-is and accept the small over-pass. Cosmetic refactor, low-priority.

findings.description: Root layout now calls headers() and is marked async, which opts every route inheriting the layout into dynamic rendering. Verified in .next/server/app: robots.txt and sitemap.xml remain Static (they are metadata routes and don't inherit the root layout's tree), but every page under / and /en now renders on every request. For a portfolio with DB-backed home pages (which are already dynamic because of the Supabase fetch) this is acceptable, but it's worth noting.
findings.fix: No action required given the current data-fetching pattern. If static-generation of the locale shells becomes a goal later, replace the headers()-driven <html lang> with a [locale] route group (/[locale]/page.tsx) or hoist the <html> element into per-route layouts (possible in Next 16 via the nested layout escape hatch). Document the trade-off in CLAUDE.md if it becomes ambiguous for future agents.

verified_non_issues: proxy.ts: forwardedHeaders.set("x-pathname", request.nextUrl.pathname) OVERWRITES any client-supplied x-pathname header. No .append, no read-before-set. The cookie-refresh rebuild inside the Supabase setAll callback reuses the same forwardedHeaders instance, so the pathname is never lost mid-flow. Safe against header injection.

getDictionary uses `DICTIONARIES[locale] ?? DICTIONARIES[DEFAULT_LOCALE]` — null-safe even if an unknown string is passed; Locale union is compile-time narrow so runtime strings from a future query-param toggle still fall back cleanly.
getLocaleFromPathname: string-prefix check `pathname === "/en" || pathname.startsWith("/en/")` — no regex, no user-input eval, safe.

<html lang> flip: verified that both / and /en routes set lang correctly via the x-pathname header, and the fallback to pt-BR when the header is missing is the correct behaviour for metadata routes that slip through the matcher (sitemap/robots don't use the layout at all, so the fallback is moot for them but still defensive).
i18n pt.ts and en.ts both satisfy Dictionary; TypeScript enforces structural parity — no missing keys. Semantic translations look reasonable on a spot check.

ProjectsCarousel.selectDescription: correct locale preference + fallback + null handling → dict.emptyDescription placeholder. key={safeIndex} (not github_id) is used intentionally to remount the reveal animation — acceptable because the inner list is stack.map using key={tech}.

readme.ts parser: bilingual split via /^##\s+English\s*$/i is case-insensitive and anchored; parseEnglishDescription bounds its scan to 8 lines (no ReDoS risk); stack parsing scoped to ptLines so EN-side ### Stack can't leak; joinParagraph shared with PT path so both obey MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH=500.
sync.ts: missing_en_description counter increments post-decision so both fresh and cached rows contribute; insert path writes null (not '') for missing EN; is_hidden logic unchanged.

SQL migration: idempotent DO $$ block on the rename (information_schema check); ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS on description_en; wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT. Safe on a production replica (Supabase cloud is single-primary so replica concern is moot; the rename acquires an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock but the table is small — sub-second blip). RLS policies on public.repos don't reference the column name, so no policy change needed.
.next/server/app output confirms robots.txt and sitemap.xml have .body+.meta Static artifacts, so the root-layout headers() call did NOT demote them.

LocaleToggle useEffect: listener is registered once with []/no-deps, cleanup calls removeEventListener — no leak, no duplicate binding across fast next/link soft navs because the component unmounts+remounts across / ↔ /en (different React trees per route).

LocaleToggle unmount on scene 4 is the correct pattern per WCAG APG — aria-hidden wrapping focusable descendants would be a violation. Unmounting removes Tab targets entirely. Scene 4 focus lands inside the form (first input is email), so no focus-lost mid-Tab. Skip link remains the first focusable element on load on both / and /en.
